name: Card table — open deck
description: A felt table and a shuffled deck. Tap the pile to deal cards onto the table, then move them however your game demands. Powered by the cardsapi.com / CardForge deck API — swap in your own CardForge project to deal a custom deck.

rules.objective: There is no built-in game — this is an open card table. Bring your own rules (poker, scopa, tarot, a homebrew TCG…) and use the deck, chips and turn counter as a shared surface.
rules.setup: Tap the deck pile to deal cards onto the table. Drag chips from the tray to bet or mark. Use the turn counter to keep order.
howToPlay: Tap the face-down pile to draw the top card from the shared, server-shuffled deck — everyone draws from the same deck in the same order.
Drag dealt cards around the table; drag to the 🗑️ to discard.
Press ⟳ on the pile to shuffle a fresh deck.
Drag chips from the tray to bet, score or mark.
rules.winning: Whatever your house rules say — the table enforces nothing.
variants: Point the deck at your own CardForge project (`decks[].cardforge: "org/repo"`) to deal a custom deck with rendered art.

context.period: Cards reached Europe in the 14th century
context.blurb: Playing cards are a late arrival — they spread from China through the Islamic world to Europe only in the 1370s, more than a thousand years after the dice and board games of antiquity. This table deals from cardsapi.com, a deckofcardsapi.com-compatible API: a standard 52-card deck by default, or any deck you design in CardForge.
